purpose: Apply the certified Revenue Phase 1 and Phase 1.5 migrations, activate Anchor-only read capabilities, execute exactly one controlled Eliza Bulger lifecycle, disable operator writes immediately afterward, reconcile, prove deterministic reconstruction, and produce certification evidence.
approved_at_policy: Intentionally unset. Must be set to the actual UTC finalization time by Jacob Maynard at signing; never pre-filled.

freeze_scope: no manual Anchor runtime configuration change outside the authorized revenue flag sequence
no campaign, sender, provider, follow-up, or retention activation
no automatic continuation beyond Revenue Phase 1.6B
window.rebinding_policy: This window and the release identity below must be re-bound to fresh observed values after the root-cause-fix PR merges to protected main; any rebinding recomputes authorization_hash.

release.protected_main_commit_source: git ls-remote origin main after PR #29 merge

deployed_commit_verification.note: Fresh post-merge identity: protected main equals active SUCCESS deployment commit fcb88c6 after PR #29.

release.identity_policy: At finalization and immediately before execution, re-read the active Railway production deployment. deployed_commit must equal protected_main_commit and railway_deployment_id must identify the active successful pulseforge-leadgen production deployment. Phase 1.6A identity is reference evidence only and cannot substitute for a fresh check.

migration_checksums.verification: phase1 checksum re-certified after the production-compatibility fix (tenant composite keys provisioned idempotently; campaigns FK conditional). phase15 and rollback unchanged. All three verified against the release branch; re-verify against protected main after merge.

phase16a_prerequisite.retention: 90 days after Phase 1.6 completion or explicit operator disposition; no early deletion

canary.prospect_id_policy: Intentionally null. Eliza Bulger is created as a new customer; no historical prospect ID may be fabricated, inferred, or substituted.
canary.job_note: Inspected the main bedroom for evidence of pet spray using a UV light. No evidence was found. The landlord requested the service proceed regardless. Job completed and Eliza Bulger paid $150 in full.

operator_only_runtime_values.customer_primary_email_status: APPROVED_NULL: Jacob Maynard explicitly approved null; contact information is unknown and must not be fabricated (2026-07-21).
operator_only_runtime_values.customer_primary_phone_status: APPROVED_NULL: Jacob Maynard explicitly approved null; contact information is unknown and must not be fabricated (2026-07-21).
operator_only_runtime_values.service_address_status: APPROVED_NULL: Jacob Maynard explicitly approved null; service address is unknown and must not be fabricated (2026-07-21).
operator_only_runtime_values.scheduled_start_status: UNRESOLVED_NON_EXECUTABLE: supply {local_date, timezone:"America/New_York", precision:"day", operator_confirmed:true}. Exact clock time is not required and must not be fabricated.
operator_only_runtime_values.completion_date_status: UNRESOLVED_NON_EXECUTABLE: supply {local_date, timezone:"America/New_York", precision:"day", operator_confirmed:true}. Exact clock time is not required and must not be fabricated.
operator_only_runtime_values.payment_received_at_status: UNRESOLVED_NON_EXECUTABLE: supply {local_date, timezone:"America/New_York", precision:"day", operator_confirmed:true}. Exact clock time is not required and must not be fabricated.
operator_only_runtime_values.payment_method_status: UNRESOLVED_NON_EXECUTABLE: job note evidences payment in full but not the method; must be supplied by Jacob Maynard.
operator_only_runtime_values.estimated_direct_cost_cents_status: APPROVED_NULL: direct cost is unknown; operator directed null rather than a fabricated figure (2026-07-21).
operator_only_runtime_values.actual_direct_cost_cents_status: APPROVED_NULL: direct cost is unknown; operator directed null rather than a fabricated figure (2026-07-21).
operator_only_runtime_values.idempotency_key_policy: Immutable once written here. Bound to authorization 3808a9f7-b8e4-467f-917f-5021dfb7d485 and correlation bc286f24-f816-41bc-bdd0-a874da7b11eb. Any regeneration invalidates the authorization hash and requires re-approval. The eight keys of retired authorization ce9005f1 are permanently non-reusable.

flag_sequence.state: all revenue environment and database flags off
Anchor-only revenue schema, operator reads, and Max reads enabled; operator writes and follow-up recommendations off
flag_sequence.required_before: read-only verification and baseline compare-only reconciliation

flag_sequence.state: Anchor-only operator writes enabled temporarily; follow-up recommendations remain off

flag_sequence.required_before: recorded reconciliation, deterministic reconstruction proof, certification, and window close

deterministic_reconstruction.method: Run the non-destructive projection reconstruction comparison twice against the same fixed client-10 event boundary after writes are disabled. Canonicalize away run identifiers and timestamps. Require identical reconstructed projection hashes, exact equality with the persisted projection, zero mismatches, and zero unexplained events.

stop_conditions: authorization validation fails or authorization is unsigned, unhashed, not yet active, or expired
protected-main commit, deployed commit, Railway deployment identity, migration path, or migration checksum differs
Phase 1.6A durable backup, readability, retention, Keychain, restore, or independent-verification evidence is unavailable or differs
production environment, database, client 10, or authorized actor cannot be conclusively identified
any revenue flag is enabled before its authorized sequence step or any non-client-10 revenue flag changes
schema, constraint, permission, trigger, append-only, route, application, database, or connection health verification fails
any one-cent discrepancy appears in booked, delivered, collected, refunded, or net-collected revenue
ledger event count is not exactly 12 or projected/source outcome count is not exactly 1
tenant mismatch, duplicate financial effect, duplicate outcome attempt, idempotency mismatch, or unauthorized record appears
append-only violation, unexplained event, source/ledger/projection mismatch, or reconciliation failure occurs
deterministic reconstruction hashes differ from each other or from the persisted projection
refund, external communication, provider activity, follow-up creation or send, retention activity, or Max mutation occurs
operator writes are not disabled immediately after the one canary attempt, including on failure
application or database health degrades or migration result is ambiguous
any request is made to continue automatically beyond Revenue Phase 1.6B

rollback_policy.migration_failure: Stop immediately. Do not retry or infer success. Preserve output and follow the certified operational rollback only under the named rollback owner.
rollback_policy.post_migration_failure: Keep all flags off. Preserve evidence. Do not run the canary.
rollback_policy.canary_failure: Disable operator writes immediately. Do not delete or mutate append-only revenue events. Use only separately reviewed compensating-event procedures if later authorized.

certification_evidence_required: final signed authorization and canonical authorization hash
fresh protected-main, Railway deployment, migration checksum, and production environment identity evidence
migration output and post-migration schema, constraint, permission, trigger, append-only, and health evidence
initial, transient, and final feature-flag state evidence scoped to client 10
all Eliza customer, opportunity, job, payment, outcome, ledger, audit, idempotency, and correlation identifiers
two deterministic reconstruction hashes and persisted projection hash
no external communication, provider activity, follow-up, retention, other-client write, refund, second canary, or Max mutation evidence
final COMPLETE or BLOCKED verdict with any stop condition

execution_readiness.ready_to_sign_blockers: Root-cause-fix PR must merge through protected main with revenue-postgresql-required passing on the exact release commit; release identity and window must then be re-bound to the fresh deployment. Operator and Founder attestations, actual UTC approved_at, and finalization via the offline finalizer remain outstanding. Confirmed operator facts (scheduled/completion/payment dates 2026-07-14 America/New_York day precision; payment method stripe_card) are supplied to the finalizer at signing, not written into this unsigned draft. Independent human review is not a required gate under the certified solo-founder zero-approval policy.
repository_gaps_requiring_offline_implem: SUPERSEDED: earlier exploration concluded the Phase 1.6B validator, runner, historical-date derivation, and reconstruction verifier were missing. Those components are now implemented; see implemented_components and docs/REVENUE_PHASE16B_IMPLEMENTATION_REPORT.md.

SUPERSEDED: independent human review is not a required gate under the certified solo-founder policy (zero approvals). The repository gate is protected PR merge with mandatory revenue-postgresql-required passing on the exact release commit.

The current authorization intentionally remains unsigned, unapproved, and non-executable until Jacob supplies attestations and finalizes via the offline finalizer after the release commit is on protected main.
execution_readiness.rule: Implemented components do not themselves authorize execution. Protected-main merge with revenue-postgresql-required, signatures, approved_at, finalization, active window, fresh identity observation, and REVENUE_PHASE16B_PRODUCTION_ENABLED=true remain required. Independent human review is not required.

remaining_operator_only_values: authorized_operator.signature (Jacob Maynard's personal attestation; cannot be generated)
approving_authority.signature (Jacob Maynard's personal attestation as Founder; cannot be generated)
approved_at using the actual UTC time at signing
canary.operator_only_runtime_values.scheduled_start as an operator-confirmed America/New_York local_date with day precision
canary.operator_only_runtime_values.completion_date as an operator-confirmed America/New_York local_date with day precision
canary.operator_only_runtime_values.payment_received_at as an operator-confirmed America/New_York local_date with day precision
final authorization_hash recomputed after signatures, approved_at, and all runtime values are fixed
values_deferred_to_the_active_window_not: deterministic_reconstruction.fixed_event_boundary (bound only after the post-migration baseline is observed inside the window)
execution-time re-observation of release.deployed_commit and release.railway_deployment_id per release.identity_policy
authorization_hash_policy: authorization_hash below is the canonical SHA-256 of this exact unsigned draft with authorization_hash excluded and every object key recursively sorted. It binds the generated IDs, window, window owners, deployment identity, historical-date precision policy, and canary facts against tampering. It is NOT the final signing hash: it must be recomputed after signatures, approved_at, and the remaining runtime values are fixed, and the final hash supersedes it.

superseded_authorization.outcome: BLOCKED SAFELY; production unchanged; no migration, flag change, or Eliza record
superseded_authorization.initiating_error: SQLSTATE 42830: there is no unique constraint matching given keys for referenced table "companies" (phase1 migration, CREATE TABLE customers composite tenant FK); obscured in evidence by secondary 25P02 from a shutdown probe on the same aborted connection
superseded_authorization.reuse_policy: The retired authorization ID, correlation ID, idempotency keys, window, and signed document are permanently non-reusable and are rejected by the validator.
